reforms.description: Sweden has significantly increased its national income dedicated to research and development (R&D), reaching almost 4% of GDP by the mid-1990s. In 1998, the government established a government-funded energy research, development and demonstration programme with a budget of SKr5 billion over seven years to promote an ecologically-sustainable energy system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D and targeted energy research is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding programme has been established and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant government-funded programme represents a major structural change in R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: A government-funded energy research, development and demonstration programme to promote an ecologically-sustainable energy system was established at the beginning of 1998 with funding of SKr5 billion over seven years.

reforms.description: Public funding of research into environmental matters is prioritized, with significant taxpayer financing aimed at understanding environmental damage and its causes, contributing to international scientific understanding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in environmental research enhances scientific knowledge and can lead to innovations that address environmental challenges.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and is actively supporting research.
reforms.importance_rationale: The prioritization of public funding for environmental research represents a significant but narrower reform.
reforms.source_quote: Public funding of research into environmental matters is also a high policy priority.

reforms.description: Sweden has introduced a system of differentiated fairway dues to provide economic incentives for shipping companies to reduce sulphur and nitrogen oxide emissions, encouraging the use of cleaner fuels and technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The introduction of economic incentives is expected to stimulate innovation in cleaner technologies within the shipping industry.
reforms.status_evidence: The system of differentiated fairway dues is currently operational and incentivizing cleaner practices.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of differentiated fairway dues represents a significant reform in environmental policy aimed at innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Sweden has introduced a system of differentiated fairway dues to provide economic incentives to reduce emissions from shipping.
